title: How to adopt AI work tools inside the apps your team already uses
description: A practical guide to using AI inside docs, spreadsheets, chat and connected files. It explains where the gains come from, why logs and approval steps matter, and what to check before rolling it out.
search_question: How should a team adopt AI work tools inside the apps it already uses?

revisions.summary: Created a durable explainer on adopting AI work tools inside existing apps, focusing on workflow fit, human review, permissions, and current examples from OpenAI, Microsoft, Anthropic, and Google.

Updated the guide to reflect current in-app AI adoption examples from ChatGPT Work, Microsoft 365 Copilot, Slack-based Claude usage, and Google ad disclosure. Added clearer guidance on permissions, review steps, rollout uncertainty, and the difference between projected benefits and measured outcomes.

Updated the existing work-tools guide to reflect the new shift from standalone AI assistants to embedded, agentic workflows that require logs, approval gates and rollback plans. Added current examples from OpenAI, Microsoft, Google, Anthropic and the White House, and clarified rollout uncertainty, projected benefits versus measured outcomes, and the practical questions teams should ask before enabling AI inside their existing apps.

editorial_basis: Generated only from Clearforge research packs that had already passed publication approval and material-claim verification.
